title: Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam
tagline: Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Pazhamalai Nadhar Temple is a Hindu temple in the town of Virudhachalam, Cuddalore district of Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: The Pazhamalai Nadhar Temple is a Hindu temple in the town of Virudhachalam, Cuddalore district of Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity PazhamalaiNadhar is revered in Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the nayanmars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam. The temple gives its name to the town of Virudhachalam. The temple is famed for the legend of Shiva forming as a mountain heeding to the prayers of Brahma.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (80 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (79 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam?
faq.a: Stays in Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vriddhagiriswarar Temple, Vriddhachalam?
faq.a: The top attractions are Asaveerankudikkadu, Ayanathathanur, Vanjinapuram, Manakkudaiyan, Ponparappi.
